question: Create a `PrivateData` class that has a private integer member `value`. Declare an external `friend` function `displayPrivateValue(const PrivateData& obj)` that can access and print `obj.value`.

test_cases.expected:
Attempting to display uninitialized private value:
Private Value: [indeterminate_value]
Attempting to display initialized private value:
Private Value: 200

errors: The `PrivateData` class lacks an explicit constructor to initialize its `value` member. When `PrivateData p;` is created, `p.value` remains uninitialized, leading to undefined behavior (printing a garbage value) when `displayPrivateValue(p)` is called before `setValue()` is invoked. This is a common robustness issue in C++ where objects are used without proper initialization.
